input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText:
The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate it to `.runsettings` after upgrading MSTest packages. Let me first look at their files to understand the current configuration, then provide guidance.

Let me check the files in their project.

data.content:
`.testsettings` is a legacy format — **MSTest v3 ignores it**. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the mapping from your current settings:

| `.testsettings` | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout=30000` | `<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` in `<MSTest>` |
| Code coverage DataCollector | `<DataCollectors>` section |
| `AssemblyResolution` / deployment | `<DeploymentEnabled>` (usually no longer needed) |

Let me create the `.runsettings` and update your project:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Timeout per test in milliseconds (was TestTimeout in .testsettings) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test method timeout; equivalent to the .testsettings TestTimeout property -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Deployment is handled automatically in modern MSTest; enable only if needed -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file that configures:
1. Assembly resolution / test deployment
2. Test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. Code coverage data collector

They're on MSTest 3.8.0 targeting net8.0. The `.testsettings` format is legacy and not supported by modern MSTest. They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file.

Let me create the equivalent `.runsettings` file and update the `.csproj` to reference it.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Timeout per test in milliseconds (was TestTimeout in .testsettings) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test method timeout; equivalent to the .testsettings TestTimeout property -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Deployment is handled automatically in modern MSTest; enable only if needed -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
Here's what was done:

1. **Created `local.runsettings`** with equivalent settings:
   - **TestTimeout** → `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` (per-test method timeout)
   - **Code coverage** → modern `DataCollector` with `CodeCoverage/2.0` URI
   - **Deployment** → `DeploymentEnabled=false` (not needed in modern MSTest; re-enable if your tests depend on deployed files)

2. **Updated `.csproj`** with `<RunSettingsFilePath>` so the settings are automatically picked up.

You can now **delete `local.testsettings`** — it is no longer used. You can also pass the runsettings explicitly via CLI:

```
dotnet test --settings local.runsettings
```
